Fixture chrome

SPA Navigation Fixture

PageMint should preserve this article as a stable paginated export without repeating the fixed header chrome or losing the main article container.

This fixture exists to exercise the browser boundary rather than the exact visual output, so the content is intentionally simple but long enough to trigger meaningful DOM preparation.

The export path still needs a real page, real scripting injection, and a real Chrome DevTools Protocol session; otherwise we only prove that our mocks agree with themselves.

A production browser harness is supposed to catch exactly the failures that mocked executeScript calls hide, including context loss, navigation drift, and malformed page-state returns.
